rationale.root_cause: At the FLAW site (around line 42), memcpy uses sizeof(*structCharVoid) as the length while writing to structCharVoid->charFirst (16 bytes), overflowing into voidSecond and voidThird. This corrupts the pointer fields and triggers a heap buffer overflow when the corrupted pointer is later used.
rationale.local_fix: Change the memcpy length to sizeof(structCharVoid->charFirst) so only the character field is written. This prevents overwriting the adjacent pointer members.
rationale.global_fix: Introduce a bounded field-copy helper or typed buffer wrapper that ties the destination array to its capacity (e.g., copy_cstr_to_array(dst)), avoiding raw size arguments. This design prevents confusing struct size with field size.
